title_en: The Creation and Nature of Jinn
content_en:
The jinn are a category of sentient beings whose existence is affirmed in the Quran and Sunnah and whose nature differs fundamentally from both angels and human beings. Al-Ashqar's comprehensive study of this world provides what is perhaps the most systematic treatment in the modern English-language Islamic literature, drawing on the Quran, the authenticated prophetic traditions, and the classical scholarly heritage to present a theologically sound and intellectually rigorous account.

Allah created the jinn from 'smokeless flame of fire' (marij min nar), as established in the Quran (Ar-Rahman: 15). This means they are beings of a fiery energy, analogous to how human beings are beings of clay (having been created from the earth). Their origin from fire correlates with some of the characteristics attributed to them: their ability to move with great speed, their penetration of spaces inaccessible to humans, and their capacity for strong passions. Just as the human being's earthly origin is associated with stability, weight, and material vulnerability, the jinn's fiery origin is associated with mobility, subtlety, and a kind of passionate energy.

The jinn are invisible to human beings in their natural state. This invisibility is a specific feature of their nature as Al-Ashqar notes, drawing on the Quranic verse: 'He sees you, he and his tribe, from where you do not see them' (Al-A'raf: 27) — stated in the context of Iblis and his progeny, confirming the asymmetric visibility between jinn and humans. However, jinn can take on forms visible to humans, including animal forms and human forms, in certain circumstances.

Like human beings, jinn are mukallaf — morally responsible beings subject to divine command and prohibition. They have free will, can believe or disbelieve, can be righteous or corrupt, and will be judged on the Day of Judgment. The entire Surah Al-Jinn describes a group of jinn who heard the Quran recited and immediately believed, declaring: 'We have heard a wondrous recitation, it guides to righteousness, so we have believed in it.' This surah establishes that jinn have the capacity for genuine faith, are accountable for their religious choices, and will receive reward or punishment accordingly — paralleling the human moral and spiritual experience.

Jinn possess extraordinary physical capabilities compared to human beings. They can move across vast distances rapidly, as illustrated in the Quran when a jinn offers to bring the throne of Bilqis from Yemen to Sulayman in Jerusalem 'before you rise from your place.' They can carry heavy loads, inhabit places uncomfortable for humans, and access information inaccessible to ordinary human senses. These capabilities are part of their created nature, not divine powers — they remain entirely within the bounds of what Allah has granted to created beings.
content_id:
Jin adalah kategori makhluk berakal yang keberadaannya ditegaskan dalam Al-Quran dan Sunnah dan yang hakikatnya berbeda secara fundamental dari malaikat maupun manusia. Kajian komprehensif al-Asyqar tentang alam ini memberikan apa yang mungkin merupakan perlakuan paling sistematis dalam literatur Islam berbahasa Indonesia modern, mengacu pada Al-Quran, tradisi-tradisi kenabian yang terotentikasi, dan warisan kesarjanaan klasik untuk menyajikan akun yang sahih secara teologis dan ketat secara intelektual.

Allah menciptakan jin dari nyala api yang tidak berasap (marij min nar), sebagaimana ditetapkan dalam Al-Quran (Ar-Rahman: 15). Ini berarti mereka adalah makhluk dari energi yang berapi, analog dengan bagaimana manusia adalah makhluk dari tanah liat (karena diciptakan dari bumi). Asal mereka dari api berkorelasi dengan beberapa karakteristik yang dikaitkan kepada mereka: kemampuan mereka untuk bergerak dengan kecepatan tinggi, menembus ruang-ruang yang tidak dapat diakses manusia, dan kapasitas mereka untuk hasrat yang kuat. Sama seperti asal manusia dari bumi dikaitkan dengan stabilitas, berat, dan kerentanan material, asal jin dari api dikaitkan dengan mobilitas, kehalusan, dan sejenis energi yang bergairah.

Jin tidak terlihat oleh manusia dalam keadaan alami mereka. Ketidaktampakan ini adalah fitur spesifik dari hakikat mereka sebagaimana al-Asyqar catat, mengacu pada ayat Al-Quran: Dia melihat kalian, dia dan sukunya, dari tempat yang kalian tidak dapat melihatnya (Al-A'raf: 27), dinyatakan dalam konteks Iblis dan keturunannya, mengkonfirmasi visibilitas asimetris antara jin dan manusia. Namun, jin dapat mengambil bentuk yang terlihat oleh manusia, termasuk bentuk hewan dan bentuk manusia, dalam keadaan tertentu.

Seperti manusia, jin adalah mukallaf, yaitu makhluk yang bertanggung jawab secara moral yang tunduk pada perintah dan larangan ilahi. Mereka memiliki kehendak bebas, dapat beriman atau kafir, dapat saleh atau jahat, dan akan diadili pada Hari Kiamat. Seluruh Surah Al-Jinn menggambarkan sekelompok jin yang mendengar Al-Quran dibacakan dan segera beriman, menyatakan: Sungguh kami telah mendengar bacaan yang menakjubkan, ia membimbing kepada kebenaran, maka kami telah beriman kepadanya. Surah ini menetapkan bahwa jin memiliki kapasitas untuk iman yang tulus, bertanggung jawab atas pilihan-pilihan keagamaan mereka, dan akan menerima pahala atau hukuman sebagaimana mestinya, sejajar dengan pengalaman moral dan spiritual manusia.

Jin memiliki kemampuan fisik yang luar biasa dibandingkan manusia. Mereka dapat bergerak melintasi jarak yang sangat jauh dengan cepat, seperti yang diilustrasikan dalam Al-Quran ketika seorang jin menawarkan untuk membawa singgasana Bilqis dari Yaman ke tempat Sulayman di Yerusalem sebelum kamu bangkit dari tempatmu. Mereka dapat membawa beban berat, menghuni tempat-tempat yang tidak nyaman bagi manusia, dan mengakses informasi yang tidak dapat dijangkau oleh indera manusia biasa. Kemampuan-kemampuan ini adalah bagian dari hakikat ciptaan mereka, bukan kekuatan ilahi, mereka tetap sepenuhnya dalam batas-batas apa yang Allah berikan kepada makhluk-makhluk yang diciptakan.
title_ar: عالم الجن — حقيقة الجن في القرآن والسنة
content_ar: The الجن are a category of sentient beings whose existence is affirmed in the القرآن الكريم and السنة and whose nature differs بشكل أساسي from both الملائكة and human beings. Al-Ashqar's comprehensive study of this الدنيا provides what is perhaps the most systematic treatment in the modern English-language Islamic literature, drawing on the القرآن الكريم, the authenticated prophetic traditions, and the الكلاسيكي العلمي heritage to present a theologically sound and intellectually rigorous account. الله created the الجن from 'smokeless flame of fire' (marij min nar), as established in the القرآن الكريم (Ar-Rahman: 15). This means they are beings of a fiery energy, analogous to how human beings are beings of clay (having been created from the earth). Their origin from fire correlates with some of the characteristics attributed to them: their ability to move with great speed, their penetration of spaces inaccessible to humans, and their capacity for strong passions. Just as the human being's earthly origin is associated with stability, weight, and material vulnerability, the الجن's fiery origin is associated with mobility, subtlety, and a kind of passionate energy. The الجن are invisible to human beings in their natural state. This invisibility is a specific feature of their nature as Al-Ashqar notes, drawing on the القرآني الآية: 'He sees you, he and his tribe, from where you do not see them' (Al-A'raf: 27) — stated in the context of إبليس and his progeny, confirming the asymmetric visibility between الجن and humans. غير أن, الجن can take on forms visible to humans, بما في ذلك animal forms and human forms, in certain circumstances. Like human beings, الجن are mukallaf — morally responsible beings subject to divine command and prohibition. They have free will, can believe or disbelieve, can be الصالح or corrupt, and will be judged on the يوم القيامة. The entire السورة Al-الجن describes a group of الجن who heard the القرآن الكريم recited and immediately believed, declaring: 'We have heard a wondrous التلاوة, it guides to righteousness, لذا we have believed in it.' This السورة establishes that الجن have the capacity for genuine الإيمان, are accountable for their religious choices, and will receive الأجر or العقاب وبناءً على ذلك — paralleling the human moral and spiritual experience. الجن possess extraordinary physical capabilities compared to human beings. They can move across vast distances rapidly, as illustrated in the القرآن الكريم عندما a الجن offers to bring the throne of Bilqis from Yemen to سليمان ﷺ in Jerusalem 'قبل you rise from your place.' They can carry heavy loads, inhabit places uncomfortable for humans, and access information inaccessible to ordinary human senses. These capabilities are part of their created nature, not divine powers — they remain entirely within the bounds of what الله has granted to created beings.
